name: read_file
description: Read the entire contents of a file. Returns the file content as text, or an error if the file does not exist.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file to read.

name: write_file
description: Write content to a file, creating it if it does not exist or overwriting if it does. Use with caution.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file to write.

content.description: The content to write to the file.

name: edit_file
description: Make a targeted edit to a file by replacing one string with another. Only the first match is replaced. Use this for surgical changes instead of write_file when possible.

path.description: Path to the file to edit.

newString.description: The text to replace it with.

name: edit_file_preview
description: Preview the diff that edit_file would produce WITHOUT applying changes. Takes the same parameters as edit_file (path, old_string, new_string) and returns a unified diff string. Use this to show the user what would change before committing.

path.description: Absolute path to the file to preview changes for

old_string.description: The exact text to find in the file

new_string.description: The text to replace it with

name: multi_edit_preview
description: Preview combined diff for multiple file edits WITHOUT applying. Takes an array of edit operations (each with path, old_string, new_string) and returns a unified diff showing all changes. Use to review multi-file changes before committing.

edits.description: Array of edit operations to preview

name: multi_edit_apply
description: Apply multiple file edits atomically — all succeed or all fail. Takes an array of edit operations. Validates all edits first, then applies them. If any edit fails validation, no files are modified.

edits.description: Array of edit operations to apply

name: list_dir
description: List the contents of a directory. Returns an array of file and directory names.

path.description: Path to the directory to list.

name: terminal_start
description: Start a persistent shell session for interactive commands

name: terminal_send
description: Send input to a terminal session and read output

name: terminal_stop
description: Stop and clean up a terminal session

name: terminal_list
description: List all active terminal sessions

name: run_command
description: Execute a shell command in the workspace directory and return stdout + stderr. Requires user permission.

workdir.description: Optional working directory for the command. Defaults to the workspace root.

name: grep
description: Search file contents using a regular expression and return matching lines.

pattern.description: The regex pattern to search for.

path.description: Optional directory to search in. Defaults to the workspace root.

name: glob
description: Find files matching a glob pattern and return their paths.

path.description: Optional base directory for the search. Defaults to the workspace root.

name: search_codebase
description: Search the codebase for a symbol, function, class, type, or pattern. Returns matching locations with context, classified as definitions, imports, or references. More targeted than grep — understands code structure. Use this to find where something is defined, imported, or used.

query.description: The symbol name, function name, class name, or pattern to search for.

path.description: Root directory to search in. Use "." for the entire workspace.

name: semantic_search
description: Find code by meaning using embeddings (not exact text). Best for "where is the logic that does X", "code related to Y", or locating relevant areas when you do not know the exact name. For exact symbol/string matches prefer search_codebase or grep. Requires the workspace to have been indexed (semantic_index); returns top matches with path, line range, score, and snippet.

query.description: Natural-language description of the code you are looking for.

name: semantic_index
description: Build or refresh the semantic search index for the workspace (embeds source files). Run once before semantic_search, or after large changes. Incremental — unchanged files are skipped.

name: generate_image
description: Generate an image from a text prompt using AI. Returns a base64 data URL of the generated image. Use for creating illustrations, diagrams, or visual content.

prompt.description: Detailed description of the image to generate

style.description: Image style: natural or vivid. Default: natural

name: lsp_hover
description: Get hover information for a symbol at a specific position (type, signature, documentation). Works like an LSP hover request but uses text-based analysis. Supports TypeScript, JavaScript, Rust, and Python.

path.description: Absolute path to the source file.

name: lsp_go_to_definition
description: Find the definition location of a symbol at a specific position. Returns the file path, line, column, and surrounding context. Supports TypeScript, JavaScript, Rust, and Python.

path.description: Absolute path to the source file.

name: lsp_find_references
description: Find all references to a symbol at a specific position across the workspace. Returns up to 100 references with file path, line, column, and context. Supports TypeScript, JavaScript, Rust, and Python.

path.description: Absolute path to the source file.

include_definition.description: Whether to include the definition site in results (default: true).

name: git_status
description: Show the working tree status in short format, including current branch.

name: git_diff
description: Show unstaged changes in the working tree as a unified diff.

name: git_diff_staged
description: Show staged changes (ready to commit) as a unified diff.

name: git_log
description: Show recent commit history in one-line format with decorations.

name: git_commit
description: Stage and commit changes. Use run_command with git add first to stage files. Requires a meaningful commit message.

name: git_branch
description: Switch to an existing branch or create and switch to a new one.

name.description: Branch name to switch to or create.

create.description: If true, create the branch before switching.

name: git_push
description: Push the current branch to origin. Use after committing changes.

name: gh_pr_create
description: Create a GitHub pull request from the current branch. Requires gh CLI to be installed and authenticated. Returns the PR URL.

name: memory_read
description: Read the project memory file (.meyatu/memory.md). Contains conventions, preferences, and context that persist across sessions.

name: memory_write
description: Write to the project memory file (.meyatu/memory.md). Use it to persist durable learnings across sessions: a project convention, the user's stated preference, a non-obvious gotcha, or the fix/lesson from a problem that could recur. Keep entries short and specific. This is how you improve over time — record a lesson when you learn it rather than losing it at session end. When memory grows redundant, read it, then rewrite it with mode "replace" to merge duplicates, drop the obsolete, and regroup under headings (e.g. Conventions / Gotchas / User preferences).

content.description: The note to record (append mode), or the full curated memory (replace mode).

mode.description: append (default): add a timestamped note. replace: overwrite the whole memory file with your curated content — use it to dedup/reorganize.

name: memory_search
description: Search project memory for relevant conventions and notes using keyword matching

name: agents_rules_read
description: Read the project rules file (.meyatu/agents.yml). Returns structured coding rules (strict, suggest, policy) and a generated system prompt. Use this to understand project conventions and constraints.

name: hooks_run
description: Run lifecycle hooks (shell commands) for a given event. Hooks are configured in .meyatu/hooks.json. Events: before_tool, after_tool, before_chat, after_chat. Returns a list of hook results with exit codes, stdout/stderr, and whether execution was blocked.

event.description: Hook event: before_tool, after_tool, before_chat, or after_chat.

tool_result.description: Tool result output for after_tool events.

tool_error.description: Tool error message if the tool failed.

name: triggers_list
description: List all triggers configured in .meyatu/triggers.yml. Returns trigger definitions (file_watch, schedule, webhook) and count of active auto-run triggers.

name: triggers_watch
description: Start a single trigger (file_watch, schedule, or webhook) in the background by id. The trigger runs until the app exits. Specify the trigger id from triggers.yml.

name: triggers_start_auto
description: Start every trigger in triggers.yml whose auto_run flag is set. Use this once a workspace is opened to bring up background watchers/schedules/webhooks without starting each one individually. Returns a summary of which triggers started or failed.

name: skills_list
description: List all skills from .meyatu/skills/ directory (YAML files). Each skill provides a name, description, and category.

name: skills_load
description: Load a specific skill by name from .meyatu/skills/. Returns the full skill prompt, tool permissions, and category. Use this to adopt a specialized skill for the current task.

name: agents_list
description: List all configured parallel agents from .meyatu/agents.yml. Each agent has a name, description, system_prompt, and allowed tools. Use this to discover which specialized agents are available for parallel task execution.

name: ssh_exec
description: Execute a command on a remote server via SSH. Uses sshpass for password auth when available (falls back to key-based auth). Returns stdout, stderr, exit code, and a human-readable message. Common errors (connection refused, timeout, permission denied) are detected and explained in English.

password.description: SSH password. Requires sshpass to be installed. Omit to use key-based auth.

command.description: Command to execute on the remote host (default: hostname).

name: index_codebase
description: Scan and index the entire codebase. Returns file counts, language breakdown, package info, directory tree, entrypoints, and import graph. Use this to understand the project structure before making changes.

name: impact_analysis
description: Analyze the impact of changing a symbol (function/class/type). Given a symbol name and file, finds all callers, callees, imports, and affected files. Use this BEFORE modifying important functions to understand what will break.

symbol.description: Symbol name to analyze (function, class, type, etc.).

line.description: Line number of the definition (optional, helps disambiguate).

name: code_graph
description: Build a code graph showing all definitions (functions, classes, structs, etc.), call relationships, and imports across the codebase. Returns nodes, edges, and module info. Use this for architecture visualization.

name: test_runner
description: Detect test framework and run tests. Supports jest, vitest, cargo test, pytest, go test, maven, gradle. Returns structured results with pass/fail counts, duration, and failure details. Use optional filter to run specific tests.

name: doc_index
description: Scan and index all markdown/documentation files in the workspace. Returns file paths, titles, sections, and content structure. Use this to understand project documentation.

name: doc_search
description: Search through indexed documentation files. Returns matching sections with relevance scores and snippets. Use this to find relevant documentation.

name: error_diagnosis
description: Diagnose an error message and provide suggestions for fixing it. Matches against a library of known patterns (TypeScript, Rust, Python, Go) and returns contextual suggestions with code examples.

name: perf_analyze
description: Analyze code for performance issues. Detects N+1 queries, blocking I/O in async, unnecessary cloning, regex in loops, and other anti-patterns. Returns issues with severity and optimization suggestions.

file_path.description: Specific file to analyze (optional, analyzes all files if omitted).

name: review_add
description: Store a code review feedback item. Records file, line, comment, category (style/bug/performance/architecture/naming), and severity. Use this to track human review feedback.

category.description: Category: style, bug, performance, architecture, naming.

name: review_list
description: List all code review feedback items, optionally filtered by file or category. Returns feedbacks with stats.

name: review_resolve
description: Mark a review feedback item as resolved.

review_id.description: ID of the review to resolve.

name: review_stats
description: Get code review statistics: total/unresolved counts, breakdown by category and severity.

name: global_register_project
description: Register a project in the global memory system. Tracks language, framework, and enables cross-project learning.

framework.description: Framework if any (react, nextjs, actix, etc.).

name: global_add_note
description: Add a note to the current project memory. Categories: best_practice, gotcha, tip, architecture.

name: global_search
description: Search across all project memories. Find notes by content or tags.

name: global_add_pattern
description: Add a shared pattern learned from this project. Patterns are reusable best practices across projects.

name: dispatch_parallel_agents
description: Run several independent sub-tasks concurrently as parallel agents and get their combined results. Use for independent work that benefits from fan-out (e.g. explore N modules, review N files). Each task runs its own agent loop. Do not use for dependent/sequential work.

tasks.description: Independent sub-tasks to run in parallel.

agent.description: Optional named agent from .meyatu/agents.yml; uses its system_prompt. Omit to use the default assistant prompt.

name: web_scrape
description: Fetch a single web page and return its content as clean Markdown. Handles JavaScript-rendered pages when Chrome is available locally. Use for reading documentation, blog posts, news, or any single URL.

url.description: The full URL to scrape, including https://.

name: web_crawl
description: Crawl multiple pages from a starting URL using BFS. Each page is fetched and extracted as Markdown, separated by page URL headings. Only follows same-domain links. Use for documentation sites with multiple pages or exploring a website structure.

name: web_map
description: Discover all same-domain URLs reachable from a starting page. Returns a JSON array of URLs. Useful for understanding site structure, finding endpoints, or building a sitemap from a landing page.

url.description: Starting URL to discover links from.
